Assignment 3 — Scenario Discovery: When Do Policies Fail?

Contents

  • Learning objectives
  • Notebook
  • Key packages used
  • Submission

Assignment 3 — Scenario Discovery: When Do Policies Fail?#

Theme: PRIM · Patient Rule Induction Method · Dimensional stacking

Learning objectives#

  1. Explain the goal of scenario discovery and how it differs from optimisation.

  2. Apply PRIM to identify regions of the uncertainty space where a policy fails.

  3. Interpret PRIM output: coverage, density, and the peeling trajectory.

  4. Use dimensional stacking as a complementary visualisation of failure conditions.

  5. Communicate scenario discovery findings to a policy audience.

Notebook#

assignments_ema/assignment_03_scenario_discovery.ipynb

Key packages used#

Package

Purpose

ema_workbench

PRIM, dimensional stacking

matplotlib

Peeling trajectory and box visualisations

Submission#

Submit A3_<studentnumber>.ipynb via Brightspace with all cells executed.
